issues.body: `_dispatch_review_items` catches allocator `RuntimeError`, `SubprocessError`, and `JSONDecodeError`, prints a defer message, and continues, but it has no access to `defer_reasons` and never increments `lease-error`. Its caller only adds the returned launch count. If a tick has one or more review/fix items, every allocator claim fails because the lease ledger is unavailable, and no worker item independently records a lease error, the final summary reports `ledger=ok` and `_ledger_rot_zeroed_dispatch(...)` returns false. The claim step therefore stays green despite a zero-dispatch ledger failure. The helper-only assertions cannot detect this integration gap.
issues.fix_hint: Propagate review/fix allocator lease errors into the tick's shared `defer_reasons` (or return an equivalent error count/status from `_dispatch_review_items`) before writing the final summary and applying the fail-loud predicate. Add a non-vacuous dispatch-level self-test covering an all-review/fix frontier whose allocator raises, asserting `ledger=error` and a failing dispatch outcome; also cover mixed partial success if that is intentionally non-fatal.
issues.title: Review/fix lease failures bypass ledger health and fail-loud handling

summary: The new fail-loud behavior is incomplete. Worker-item allocator exceptions increment `lease-error`, but review/fix allocator exceptions are swallowed inside `_dispatch_review_items` without updating the shared defer histogram. Consequently, a tick whose ready frontier consists of review/fix work can suffer ledger I/O/CAS/auth failures, dispatch nothing, emit `ledger=ok`, and finish successfully—the precise silent failure this PR claims to eliminate. The added self-tests exercise only synthetic counters and do not cover that dispatch path.
